rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 28, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant BASF Corp.¿s motion for pro hac vice admission of Patrick King is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Show Cause For Failure To Serve Or In The Alternative Motion For Dismissal And Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 28, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Specially appearing defendant Van Arsdale Harris Lumber Company Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(b)(1) is denied. Although Plaintiffs failed to serve defendant within two years of commencing the personal injury action, Defendant already made a general appearance in the present action by appearing at depositions, curing defects in service. There seems to be some dispute about whether defendant answered the personal injury complaint, although the court does not see that any such answer was ever filed. Further, the court does not find prejudice to defendant. As this is a discretionary dismissal statute, the court refuses to exercise its discretion to dismiss defendant. Defendant¿s and plaintiffs¿ requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 28, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its burden of production. Plaintiff submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s objections to the testimony of Mr. Crosby and Mr. Crum are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 28, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Hollingsworth & Vose Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s fraud/deceit cause of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. On all issues, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all of plaintiff¿s evidence in support of his claims against defendant. Defendant also failed to submit affirmative evidence which negates an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims. Defendant may not incorporate and adopt another defendant¿s motion for summary judgment/adjudication. (See Frazee v. Seely (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 28, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Lorillard Tobacco Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s fraud/deceit ¿ Kent cause of action (issue 1) and claim for punitive damages (issue 2) are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden of production via the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. The court considered the entirety of Dr. Gibbs¿ declaration. Defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and Dr. Hammar¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his developing mesothelioma. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Hammar¿s declaration are overruled. As to adjudication of the fraud/deceit cause of action and plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s attempt to use its own interrogatory responses are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
